source: vidIQ IG/TikTok outlier search (live), 2 bounded calls = 10 credits; balance after ~115
finding: LIVE IN-VIDEO PERCEPTION DEMOS massively outperform self-report topics. Format beats audience: tiny accounts hit millions.

live_demo_best.note: 773x from a 280-FOLLOWER account — the format carried it entirely

troxler_pattern.claim: Troxler's-fading demos are a repeatable outlier format built from pure static motion graphics.
troxler_pattern.execution_signature: slow pacing, static text, LOW visual change, music only, LOOPED, within-an-hour effort
troxler_pattern.note: 3 independent instances clears the doctrine's 2-outlier bar for a pattern.

adjacent.rejected_for_us: requires real human faces (licensing + no-generated-humans rule)

why_beats_aphantasia: Aphantasia self-test is INTERNAL and unverifiable; no in-video moment catches the viewer out; payoff informational.
A live demo DOES something to the viewer; forced fixation IS watch time.
Pure motion graphics = our exact capability; no archive hunt, no licensing, no AI-human risk.
Every instance is LOOPED; matches the seamless-loop engine we already built.
honest_risk: SATURATION. Format-market fit proven but heavily replicated. Competitors stop at 'focus on the green circle' with NO explanation; our edge must be premium dark-editorial execution plus a real transferable payoff (WHY it fades), not the trick.
_discipline: CANDIDATE external evidence. Topic/packaging guidance only. Never promoted to approved-patterns.json or the rules DB until Curio's own platform-separated analytics confirm.
